import java.util.Arrays;
import java.util.Random;

class Solution {
    private int[] prefixSums;
    private int total;
    private Random rand;

    public Solution(int[] w) {
        prefixSums = new int[w.length];
        prefixSums[0] = w[0];
        for (int i = 1; i < w.length; i++) {
            prefixSums[i] = prefixSums[i-1] + w[i];
        }
        total = prefixSums[w.length - 1];
        rand = new Random();
    }
    
    public int pickIndex() {
        int target = rand.nextInt(total) + 1;
        int index = Arrays.binarySearch(prefixSums, target);
        if (index < 0) {
            index = -index - 1;
        }
        return index;
    }
}

solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    private int[] prefixSums;
    private int total;
    private java.util.Random rand;

    public Solution(int[] w) {
        prefixSums = new int[w.length];
        prefixSums[0] = w[0];
        for (int i = 1; i < w.length; i++) {
            prefixSums[i] = prefixSums[i-1] + w[i];
        }
        total = prefixSums[w.length - 1];
        rand = new java.util.Random();
    }
    
    public int pickIndex() {
        int target = rand.nextInt(total) + 1;
        int index = java.util.Arrays.binarySearch(prefixSums, target);
        if (index < 0) {
            index = -index - 1;
        }
        return index;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n) for initialization, O(log n) for pickIndex
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a prefix sum array to map random numbers to indices based on their weights. Binary search ensures efficient index lookup during random selection. Strengths include optimal time complexity for multiple calls to pickIndex. Weaknesses are the O(n) space for the prefix array.
